## [00:00 - 03:05]

So this lesson is all about making your launch evergreen. In the last lesson we talked about the different types of evergreen models you can use. This one is mostly about taking your actual pre-launch content and well your entire launch and your entire offer and even your product and making those things evergreen. But it's mostly the videos and the offer. A few other considerations you want to think about is comments. So if you have people leaving comments below your videos those comments might have dates on them. And this comes down to usually you don't want to restart your comment thread because you might have from your internal launch, your JV launch that you already did you've got a lot of comments there and that's great social proof. What you can usually do is set those comments so the most recent comments are at the top. And that way people will just see the most recent comments and they're not going to scroll down and find a comment that's like a year old. So another thing that sometimes this is something we do nowadays it's a more advanced thing. But on our order page there'll be actually a little chat bot so people can have a chat session or a texting session or an IM type of session with your support people. So if you're going to have a chat you got to think about how you're going to man that chat during Evergreen. For me if we do one big launch a year we know during that one week of open cart there's going to be a lot of people coming through that order page so I've got my entire team on standby waiting to man that chat. But at Evergreen you're probably going to have smaller numbers so are you going to have people watching that chat all the time. And then another one is just overall support. You need to think about your customer service or your customer support and having them ready to support Evergreen if you've got Evergreen going on a nonstop basis.
